name: cluster-management
description: Monitor and manage Proxmox cluster nodes, resources, and infrastructure health. Track node status, cluster quorum, and resource allocation across your virtualization platform.
capabilities: List all nodes in Proxmox cluster with current status
Retrieve detailed node health metrics including CPU, memory, and storage
Query cluster-wide resource utilization and allocation
Monitor cluster quorum status and configuration
Identify resource bottlenecks and capacity issues
author: DataKnifeAI
securitySummary: This skill provides documentation for Proxmox VE cluster management tools. Static analysis detected 40 pattern matches, but all are false positives: weak crypto warnings are misidentified cluster terminology, backticks are markdown formatting, and URL references are legitimate GitHub sources. The skill interacts with Proxmox infrastructure APIs which inherently requires network access and external commands.
valueStatement: Managing Proxmox cluster infrastructure requires constant monitoring of node health, resource allocation, and quorum status. This skill provides comprehensive cluster visibility and health reporting through simple natural language queries.
userTitle: Monitor Proxmox Cluster Health and Infrastructure
limitations: Requires active Proxmox VE cluster with API access configured
Read-only monitoring capabilities, does not modify cluster configuration
Depends on Proxmox MCP server for API connectivity
Cannot perform cluster maintenance or node operations

useCases.title: Daily Cluster Health Monitoring
useCases.description: Infrastructure administrators can quickly assess cluster status, verify all nodes are online, and identify any performance degradation or capacity issues before they impact workloads.

useCases.title: Capacity Planning Analysis
useCases.description: DevOps teams can analyze current resource utilization patterns across the cluster to plan for scaling, identify underutilized nodes, and optimize VM placement strategies.

useCases.title: Incident Response Investigation
useCases.description: Site reliability engineers can rapidly gather cluster state information during outages or performance incidents to identify failing nodes, resource exhaustion, or quorum problems.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Cluster Status Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the current status of all nodes in the Proxmox cluster
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick health check to verify all cluster nodes are operational

promptTemplates.title: Resource Utilization Report
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the current CPU, memory, and storage utilization across all cluster nodes?
promptTemplates.scenario: Analyzing resource consumption patterns for capacity planning

promptTemplates.title: Node Performance Deep Dive
promptTemplates.prompt: Get detailed performance metrics for node pve-01 including running VMs and resource allocation
promptTemplates.scenario: Investigating performance issues on a specific cluster node

promptTemplates.title: Comprehensive Cluster Health Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a complete cluster health report including all nodes, quorum status, resource utilization, and recommendations
promptTemplates.scenario: Executive summary for management or documentation purposes

bestPractices: Check cluster health daily and investigate any node status changes immediately
Monitor resource utilization trends to proactively plan capacity expansion before exhaustion
Maintain minimum three-node quorum for high availability and automatic failover
antiPatterns: Ignoring quorum warnings or operating cluster with insufficient nodes for failover
Running nodes at sustained high utilization without capacity planning
Making infrastructure decisions without checking current cluster resource state

faq.question: What Proxmox versions are supported?
faq.answer: This skill works with Proxmox VE 6.0 and later through the Proxmox API. Ensure your MCP server is configured with proper API credentials.

faq.question: Can this skill restart nodes or VMs?
faq.answer: No, this is a monitoring and reporting skill with read-only access. It cannot modify cluster configuration or control nodes.

faq.question: What does quorum status mean?
faq.answer: Quorum is the minimum number of nodes that must agree for cluster operations. With 3 nodes, you need 2 voting for quorum. This prevents split-brain scenarios.

faq.question: How often should I check cluster health?
faq.answer: Daily health checks are recommended. Set up monitoring alerts for critical issues, but use this skill for detailed investigation and capacity planning.

faq.question: What should I do if a node shows as offline?
faq.answer: Investigate network connectivity, check physical hardware, review Proxmox logs on the affected node, and verify cluster network configuration. VMs may need migration.

faq.question: Does this require special Proxmox permissions?
faq.answer: Yes, the API user needs read permissions for cluster resources, nodes, and status information. PVEAuditor role or equivalent is recommended.
